work_stanley

Libraries

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggmap)
library(leaflet)
library(UKFE)

Introduction

Data

In this project, we will study the factors that may affect plant nutritional quality and butterfly preference for laying eggs. We will use data from William B.V. Langdon, Richard Fox, Owen T. Lewis, Host plant use is driven by microclimate not nutritional quality in a grassland butterfly. The original research article and data sets are included in the data folder in this repository.

egg_laying <- read_csv("data/egg_laying_data.csv") %>% 
  clean_names()
## Rows: 318 Columns: 40
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (7): plant_id, species, season, gps, bare, environment_date, temperatur...
## dbl (33): predicted_n, n_se, n_weights, id_number, n_eggs, larvae, occupied,...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
plant_chemistry <- read.csv("data/plant_chemistry_data.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

Exploratory Data Analysis:

glimpse(egg_laying)
## Rows: 318
## Columns: 40
## $ predicted_n             <dbl> 2265.439, 1992.036, 1504.267, 2388.498, 2471.0…

## $ scaled_n_content        <dbl> 2.62649692, 1.55982176, -0.34319408, 3.1066056…
plant_joined <- left_join(egg_laying, plant_chemistry, by="plant_id")

Factors that may influence environmental temperature

Butterfly offspring suitability can be determined primarily by two factors: environmental temperature and plant nutritional quality.

plant_joined %>% 
  select(temperature_ambient, predicted_n, season.x) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=season.x, y=predicted_n))+
  geom_boxplot(mapping=aes(fill=season.x))

 plant_joined %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=temperature_ambient, color=season.x, fill=season.x))+
  geom_density()

 plant_joined %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=temperature_soil, color=season.x, fill=season.x))+
  geom_density()

plant_joined %>% 
  select(predicted_n, n_molar) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(predicted_n, n_molar))+
  geom_point()
## Warning: Removed 269 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

plant_joined %>% 
  select(occupied, temperature_ambient, season.x) %>% 
  mutate(occupied=factor(occupied)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=occupied))+
  geom_bar(mapping=aes(fill=season.x), color="black", position="dodge")

plant_joined %>% 
  select(occupied, predicted_n, season.x) %>% 
  mutate(occupied=factor(occupied)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=predicted_n))+
  geom_density(mapping=aes(fill=occupied), position="dodge", alpha=0.5)+
  facet_wrap(~season.x, ncol=2, labeller=label_both)
## Warning: Width not defined
## ℹ Set with `position_dodge(width = ...)`

Do butterflies prefer plants with higher percentage of bare land?

egg_laying %>% 
  filter(season=="autumn") %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(x=bare_ground, y=temperature_soil))+
  geom_point(mapping=aes(color=season))+
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=T)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

egg_laying %>% 
  filter(season=="spring") %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(x=bare_ground, y=predicted_n))+
  geom_point(mapping=aes(color=season))+
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=T)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

plant_joined %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  mutate(occupied=factor(occupied)) %>% 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(x=bare))+
  geom_bar(mapping=aes(fill=occupied), position="dodge")

ConvertGridRef(c(456531, 205539))
##   latitude longitude
## 1 51.74571  -1.18259
coordtomap <- egg_laying %>% 
  select(gps) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  separate(gps, into=c("SP", "grid"), sep=2) %>% 
  separate(grid, into=c("easting", "northing"), sep=5) %>% 
  mutate(easting=as.numeric(easting)+400000) %>% 
  mutate(northing=as.numeric(northing)+200000) %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(coords=list(ConvertGridRef(c(easting,northing)))) %>% 
  unnest(coords)
coordtomap %>% 
  summary()
##       SP               easting          northing         latitude    
##  Length:317         Min.   :456341   Min.   :205384   Min.   :51.74  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.:456412   1st Qu.:205426   1st Qu.:51.74  
##  Mode  :character   Median :456460   Median :205514   Median :51.75  
##                     Mean   :456478   Mean   :205503   Mean   :51.75  
##                     3rd Qu.:456551   3rd Qu.:205578   3rd Qu.:51.75  
##                     Max.   :456611   Max.   :205614   Max.   :51.75  
##    longitude     
##  Min.   :-1.185  
##  1st Qu.:-1.184  
##  Median :-1.184  
##  Mean   :-1.183  
##  3rd Qu.:-1.182  
##  Max.   :-1.181
